title: Structured self-critique step before returning agent output
description: The reasoning_protocol (delegate.py:1431-1442) has a VERIFY step, but it is advisory prose inside the same generation and there is no enforced self-check against the ticket's acceptance criteria before the agent commits to 'completed'. A short, explicit reflexion gate — the agent restates each acceptance criterion and marks pass/fail with evidence, and must downgrade status to needs_review if any criterion is unproven — catches over-confident 'completed' statuses that currently slip through to the reviewer Morty.
proposed_changes: Add a <self_critique> block to build_prompt() instructing the agent, after EXECUTE, to enumerate each acceptance criterion with a pass/fail verdict and one line of evidence (file:line or test name), and to set status='needs_review' if any criterion is fail/unknown. Add an optional 'criteria_check' array (criterion, verdict, evidence) to DELEGATE_OUTPUT_SCHEMA in schemas.py. In parse_agent_output(), if status=='completed' but any criteria_check verdict is not 'pass', override to 'needs_review' and audit-log the downgrade.
